repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry gives a no-solution NPIV least-squares-projection functional modulus; closest repo entries are stat_ate_overlap_decay_v1 and stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap_v1, both unrelated treatment-effect rate frontiers.

published_prior_art.cite: ShenKallusMeunierZenatiGrettonBibaut2026NoSolutionNPIV defines the no-solution inverse-problem projection target and inference setting but its arXiv abstract does not state a matching minimax modulus; CaiLow2005AdaptationConfidenceIntervals and ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics supply general modulus/inverse-problem machinery rather than this target-specific no-solution converse. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24660, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0503662, https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo proposal separates a realized no-solution NPIV functional modulus from a worst-case representer-source envelope.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR already gives exact-solution NPIR linear-functional minimax formulas under structural and representer ellipsoids; the no-solution projection interpretation is not covered. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo Stat entry states a local-Riesz square-summability root-n frontier for no-solution NPIV projection functionals.

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2022StronglyIdentifiedWeaklyIdentified shows root-n inference for some weakly identified inverse-problem functionals but does not give this singular-coordinate iff frontier for no-solution NPIV least-squares projections. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2208.08291.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry contains polynomial/severe no-solution NPIV representer-envelope phase diagrams.

published_prior_art.cite: HallHorowitz2006NonparametricMethodsIV and ChenReiss2011RateOptimalityIllPosedEconometrics give mild/severe NPIV inverse-problem rate algebra, and BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR gives exact-solution functional analogues; the no-solution projection-functional envelope is not separately stated. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0603130, https://arxiv.org/abs/0709.2003, https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo entry records this no-solution-to-exact-solution sanity reduction.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2009LocalNPIR and BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR already provide the exact-solution formulas being recovered; this proposition is a necessary reduction check, not independent novelty. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/0902.2103, https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo Stat proposal studies ordered-modulus adaptation for no-solution NPIV projection functionals.

published_prior_art.cite: BreunigJohannes2011AdaptiveFunctionalsNPIR handles adaptive exact-solution NPIR linear functionals, CaiLow2006AdaptiveLinearFunctionals supplies ordered-modulus adaptation theory, and BreunigChen2021AdaptiveQuadraticNPIV gives analogous NPIV log-loss regimes for quadratic functionals; none covers the no-solution least-squares projection target with separate projected-source and representer-source classes. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1109.0961, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0602299, https://arxiv.org/abs/2101.12282.

soundness_flags.label: thm:functional-modulus / prop:representer-envelope / prop:profile-rates
soundness_flags.one_line: The modulus and envelope need explicit finite-tail conditions: for fixed psi require the functional tail a_j psi_j to be square-summable or otherwise controlled, and for the representer envelope/profile rates require a_j b_j in l2, e.g. beta + tau > 1/2 in the polynomial case; without this, bar_R_n(b) can be infinite and the displayed polynomial rate can be non-decaying or ill-defined.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: headline no-solution NPIV converse
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The main converse is posed only in the Gaussian singular-coordinate reduction, not in the full i.i.d. conditional-moment NPIV experiment.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The field-tier novelty claim is accepted only as a conditional Gaussian-sequence minimax/modulus result for the no-solution least-squares projection target.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:adaptive-ordered-modulus
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The ordered-Lepski adaptive selector and the regime-dependent logarithmic penalties are open solve targets, not established facts.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The adaptive layer is accepted only conditional on proving both the upper adaptation bound and the claimed lower-bound necessity.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a primitive boundedness or tail condition ensuring a_j psi_j is in l2 for fixed-functionals, and add beta + tau > 1/2 or an equivalent a*b in l2 condition before stating representer-envelope profile rates.
Anchor the Shen et al. comparator to the exact theorem/equation that gives Tikhonov achievability, and anchor the Breunig-Johannes formulas being recovered in the exact-solution reduction.
For the adaptive conjecture, name the between-class modulus lower-bound route that would prove the sqrt(log n) necessity, or relabel that necessity component as a separate open-ended question.

Correct citation metadata before Stage 0: Hall-Horowitz is journal-year 2005 despite 2006 arXiv posting, and Cai-Low 'On adaptive estimation of linear functionals' is recorded by arXiv/IMS as Annals of Statistics 2005 rather than Bernoulli 2006.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the kernel clears the field novelty floor, but Stage 0 should not start until the finite-tail/rate-regime well-posedness patch is made.
